speaker: GOWER
text: In Antiochus and his daughter you have heard

speaker: GOWER
text: Of monstrous lust the due and just reward.

speaker: GOWER
text: In Pericles, his queen, and daughter seen,

speaker: GOWER
text: Although assailed with fortune fierce and keen,

speaker: GOWER
text: Virtue preserved from fell destruction’s blast,

speaker: GOWER
text: Led on by heaven, and crowned with joy at last.

speaker: GOWER
text: In Helicanus may you well descry

speaker: GOWER
text: A figure of truth, of faith, of loyalty.

speaker: GOWER
text: In reverend Cerimon there well appears

speaker: GOWER
text: The worth that learnèd charity aye wears.

speaker: GOWER
text: For wicked Cleon and his wife, when fame

speaker: GOWER
text: Had spread his cursèd deed to the honored name

speaker: GOWER
text: Of Pericles, to rage the city turn,

speaker: GOWER
text: That him and his they in his palace burn.

speaker: GOWER
text: The gods for murder seemèd so content

speaker: GOWER
text: To punish, although not done, but meant.

speaker: GOWER
text: So on your patience evermore attending,

speaker: GOWER
text: New joy wait on you. Here our play has ending.
